title: ክብረ ነገሥት — ፩
source: Carl Bezold, Kebra Nagast: Die Herrlichkeit der Könige (Munich, 1905), ch. 1 — public domain. Geʿez text reproduced (verified by checksum) from Ran HaCohen's Unicode edition of Bezold (tau.ac.il/~hacohen/KN, from Michal Jerabek's 1995 digitization). Per-sign transliteration derived mechanically from Unicode; word-level romanization auto-generated (schwa-reduced) and approximate. Transliteration convention: ś=ሠ-series, ʿ=ዐ-series, ə=6th order, order-1=ä / order-4=a. Pronunciation convention (traditional liturgical vs reconstructed) to be fixed before publication.
draftNote: DRAFT — Geʿez verified byte-identical to HaCohen's Unicode Bezold text; translations original, meaning cross-checked against Budge 1922 and Bezold's own 1905 German (they agree across the chapter). The two flagged cruxes (ተክለ ዚአሁ, ኀራን) were resolved against Dillmann's Lexicon. Remaining per-word glosses and the auto-generated word-level romanization are best-effort; readers can suggest corrections via the ⚑ button.

sentences.i: Chapter 1: Concerning the Glory of Kings
sentences.n: Chapter heading. Geʿez proofed against HaCohen's Unicode Bezold text.

sentences.t: ፍካሬ ፡ ወዜና ፡ ዘ፫፻፲ወ፰ ፡ ርቱዓነ ፡ ሃይማኖት ፡ በእንተ ፡ ክብር ፡ ወዕበይ ፡ ወተድላ ፡ ዘከመ ፡ ወሀበ ፡ እግዚኣብሔር ፡ ለደቂቀ ፡ አዳም ፤
sentences.tr: fəkare wäzena za-318 rətuʿanä haymanot bäʾnətä kəbr wäʿbäy wätädla zäkämä wähäbä ʾəgziʾabḥer lädäqiqä ʾädam
sentences.l: Interpretation and-account of-318 orthodox-of faith, concerning glory and-greatness and-delight, how gave God to-children-of Adam;
sentences.i: The interpretation and the account of the 318 orthodox in faith, concerning glory and greatness and delight — how God gave them to the children of Adam;
sentences.n: The 318 are the Fathers of the Council of Nicaea (AD 325), written with Geʿez numerals ፫፻፲ወ፰ = 300+10+8. ርቱዓነ ሃይማኖት 'orthodox of faith'. The proclitics ወ- 'and', ለ- 'to/for', በ- 'in/by', ዘ- 'which/of' recur throughout.

morph.gloss: 318 — spoken ሠለስቱ ምእት ወዐሠርቱ ወሰመንቱ (śälästu məʾət wä-ʿäśärtu wä-sämäntu); the Nicene Fathers

sentences.t: ወፈድፋደሰ ፡ ዘበእንተ ፡ ዕበያ ፡ ወክብራ ፡ ለጽዮን ፡ ታቦተ ፡ ሕጉ ፡ ለእግዚኣብሔር ፡ እንተ ፡ ገባሪሃ ፡ ወኬንያሃ ፡ ለሊሁ ፡ በውስተ ፡ ጽርሐ ፡ መቅደሱ ፡ እምቅድመ ፡ ኵሉ ፡ ፍጥረት ፡ መላእክት ፡ ወሰብእ ፡ እስመ ፡ በኅብረት ፡ ወበሥምረት ፡ ወበዕሪና ፡ ገብርዋ ፡ አብ ፡ ወወልድ ፡ ወመንፈስ ፡ ቅዱስ ፡ ለጽዮን ፡ ሰማያዊት ፡ ለማኅዳረ ፡ ስብሐቲሆሙ ፡ ወእምዝ ፡ ይቤ ፡ አብ ፡ ለወልድ ፡ ወለመንፈስ ፡ ቅዱስ ፡ ንግበር ፡ ሰብአ ፡ በአርአያነ ፡ ወበአምሳሊነ ፤
sentences.tr: wäfädfadäsä zäbäʾnətä ʿəbäya wäkbəra läṣyon tabotä ḥəgu läʾgəziʾabḥer ʾəntä gäbariha wäkenyaha lälihu bäwsətä ṣərḥä mäqdäsu ʾəmqədmä kʷəlu fəṭrät mälaʾkət wäsäbʾ ʾəsmä bäḫbərät wäbäśmərät wäbäʿrina gäbrəwa ʾäb wäwäld wämänfäs qədus läṣyon sämayawit lämaḫdarä səbḥätihomu wäʾməz yəbe ʾäb läwäld wälämänfäs qədus nəgbär säbʾä bäʾärʾäyanä wäbäʾämsalinä
sentences.l: And-especially that-concerning her-greatness and-her-glory, of-Zion, ark-of the-law of-God — of-which he-himself (is) her-maker and-her-fashioner — within hall-of his-sanctuary, before all creation, angels and-men. For in-fellowship and-in-good-will and-in-concord made-it Father and-Son and-Spirit Holy — Zion heavenly — for-dwelling-of their-glory. And-then said Father to-Son and-to-Spirit Holy, 'let-us-make man in-our-image and-in-our-likeness.'
sentences.i: and especially concerning the greatness and glory of Zion, the Ark of the Law of God, of which He Himself is the Maker and Fashioner, within the inner hall of His sanctuary, before all creation — angels and men. For in fellowship, good will, and concord the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit made her, the heavenly Zion, to be the dwelling-place of their Glory. And then the Father said to the Son and to the Holy Spirit, 'Let us make man in our image and our likeness.'
sentences.n: ጽዮን Ṣəyon (Zion) is grammatically feminine — hence 'made her', 'her maker': in the Kebra Nagast, Zion is the ታቦት tābot, the Ark/Tabernacle of the Covenant, the central symbol of the book. 'Let us make man in our image' echoes Genesis 1:26. ገብርዋ 'they made it' — the plural verb has the Trinity as subject. (Budge, Bezold's German, and this rendering agree on the sense throughout the sentence.)

morph.gloss: and especially, and the more so
and with concord, being of one accord

sentences.t: ወኀብሩ ፡ ወሠምሩ ፡ በዝ ፡ ምክር ፡ ወይቤ ፡ ወልድ ፡ አነ ፡ እለብስ ፡ ሥጋሁ ፡ ለአዳም ፤
sentences.tr: wäḫäbru wäśämru bäz məkr wäybe wäld ʾänä ʾəläbs śəgahu läʾädam
sentences.l: And-they-united and-they-consented in-this counsel, and-said the-Son, 'I, I-will-put-on body-of Adam.'
sentences.i: And they were united and of one accord in this counsel; and the Son said, 'I will put on the body of Adam.'
sentences.n: ኀብረ / ሠምረ — 'to be joined' and 'to consent, take pleasure' — recur as the verbs of the Trinity's agreement.

morph.gloss: and they consented, were pleased (root ś-m-r)
I will put on, clothe myself (imperf. 1s, l-b-s)

sentences.t: ወይቤ ፡ መንፈስ ፡ ቅዱስ ፡ አነ ፡ አኀድር ፡ ውስተ ፡ ልበ ፡ ነቢያት ፡ ወጻድቃን ፡ ወዛቲ ፡ ኅብረት ፡ ወኪዳን ፡ ተገብረት ፡ በውስተ ፡ ጽዮን ፡ ማኅደረ ፡ ስብሐቲሆሙ ፡ ወዳዊትኒ ፡ ይቤ ፡ ተዘከር ፡ ማኅበረከ ፡ ዘአቅደምከ ፡ ፈጢረ ፡ ለመድኀኒተ ፡ በትረ ፡ ርስትከ ፡ በደብረ ፡ ጽዮን ፡ ዘኀደርከ ፡ ውስቴታ ፨
sentences.tr: wäybe mänfäs qədus ʾänä ʾäḫädr wəstä ləbä näbiyat wäṣadqan wäzati ḫəbrät wäkidan tägäbrät bäwsətä ṣəyon maḫdärä səbḥätihomu wädawitni yəbe täzäkär maḫbäräkä zäʾäqdämkä fäṭirä lämädḫänitä bäträ rəstəkä bädäbrä ṣəyon zäḫädärkä wəsteta
sentences.l: And-said Spirit Holy, 'I, I-will-dwell within heart-of prophets and-righteous'; and-this union and-covenant it-was-made within Zion, dwelling-of their-glory. And-David-also said, 'Remember thy-congregation which thou-createdst-of-old for salvation, rod-of thine-inheritance, on-mount-of Zion wherein-thou-dwellest.'
sentences.i: And the Holy Spirit said, 'I will dwell in the heart of the prophets and the righteous'; and this union and covenant was made in Zion, the dwelling-place of their Glory. And David too said, 'Remember thy congregation, which thou didst create of old, for salvation — the rod of thine inheritance — on Mount Zion, wherein thou dwellest.'
sentences.n: The David quotation renders Psalm 74:2 (numbered 73:2 in the Septuagint/Vulgate, as Bezold cites). ማኅበር maḫbär is 'congregation, community' (so Bezold's 'Gemeinde' and the KJV 'congregation'), not 'covenant'; the covenant word is ኪዳን kidān just before it. ዘአቅደምከ ፈጢረ 'which thou didst create beforehand / of old'.

sentences.t: ወገብሮ ፡ ለአዳም ፡ በዘዚአሁ ፡ አርአያ ፡ ወአምሳል ፡ ከመ ፡ ይንሥቶ ፡ ለሳይጣን ፡ በእንተ ፡ ትዕቢቱ ፡ ምስለ ፡ ሰራዊቱ ፤
sentences.tr: wägäbro läʾädam bäzäziʾähu ʾärʾäya wäʾämsal kämä yənśəto läsayṭan bäʾnətä təʿbitu məslä särawitu
sentences.l: And-he-made him, Adam, after that-which (is) his-own, image and-likeness, that he-might-remove him, Satan, for his-pride, with his-host;
sentences.i: And He made Adam after His own image and likeness, that He might cast out Satan for his pride, together with his host;
sentences.n: ይንሥቶ (root n-ś-ʾ 'take, lift up') here 'remove, cast out', with the object suffix -o 'him'.

morph.gloss: in that which is his own, after his own
that he might remove/take him (n-ś-ʾ + obj)

sentences.t: ወያቅሞ ፡ ለአዳም ፡ ተክለ ፡ ዚአሁ ፡ ምስለ ፡ ኀራን ፡ ደቂቁ ፡ ለስብሐቲሁ ፡ እስመ ፡ ኅሉቅ ፡ ወምቱር ፡ ምክረ ፡ እግዚኣብሔር ፡ እንተ ፡ ይቤ ፡ እከውን ፡ ሰብአ ፡ ወኣስተርኢ ፡ ለኵሉ ፡ ዘፈጠርኩ ፡ በሥጋ ፡ እትገሰስ ፤
sentences.tr: wäyaqmo läʾädam täklä ziʾähu məslä ḫäran däqiqu läsbəḥätihu ʾəsmä ḫəluq wämtur məkrä ʾəgziʾabḥer ʾəntä yəbe ʾəkäwn säbʾä wäʾastärʾi läkʷlu zäfäṭärku bäśga ʾətgäsäs
sentences.l: and-that-he-might-establish him, Adam — plant-of his-own — with the-good his-children, for-his-glory. For determined and-decreed (was) counsel-of God, which said, 'I-will-become man, and-I-will-appear to-all which I-created; in-flesh I-will-be-handled.'
sentences.i: and that He might establish Adam — His own plant — together with the righteous, His children, for His glory. For the counsel of God was determined and decreed, in that He said, 'I will become man, and I will show myself to all that I created; in the flesh I will be handled.'
sentences.n: ተክለ ዚአሁ 'His own plant': Dillmann gives ተክል = plant / planting / plantation, so Budge's 'His own plant' is the lexical reading (Bezold's 'in his stead' is a free rendering, not the word's sense). ኀራን is the manuscript spelling of ኄራን, masc. plural of ኄር 'good, upright' (Dillmann) — 'the good/righteous', as Budge has it. ኅሉቅ ወምቱር 'numbered and decreed' is a fixed doublet for an unalterable decree.

morph.gloss: and that he might establish him (q-w-m caus. + obj)
good, upright; masc. pl. ኄራን 'the good/righteous'
and I will appear, reveal myself (r-ʾ-y caus.)
I will be touched/handled, made palpable (g-s-s)

sentences.t: ወበደኃሪ ፡ መዋዕል ፡ በሥምረቱ ፡ ተወልደ ፡ በሥጋ ፡ እምዳግማዊት ፡ ጽዮን ፡ ዳግማዊ ፡ አዳም ፡ ዝውእቱ ፡ መድኀኒነ ፡ ክርስቶስ ፡ ዛቲ ፡ ይእቲ ፡ ምክሕነ ፡ ወሃይማኖትነ ፡ ተስፋነ ፡ ወሕይወትነ ፡ ጽዮን ፡ ሰማያዊት ፨
sentences.tr: wäbädäḫari mäwaʿl bäśmərätu täwäldä bäśga ʾəmdagmawit ṣəyon dagmawi ʾädam zəwʾətu mädḫäninä kərsətos zati yəʾti məkḥənä wähaymanotnä täsfanä wäḥyəwätnä ṣəyon sämayawit
sentences.l: And-in-the-latter days, by-his-good-pleasure he-was-born in-flesh from-the-Second Zion, a-second Adam — this-is-he, our-Saviour Christ. This, she-is our-glory and-our-faith, our-hope and-our-life — Zion heavenly.
sentences.i: And in the latter days, by His good pleasure, He was born in the flesh from the Second Zion — a second Adam, who is our Saviour Christ. This is our glory and our faith, our hope and our life: the heavenly Zion.
sentences.n: ዳግማዊ አዳም 'the second Adam' = Christ; ዳግማዊት ጽዮን 'the Second Zion' is identified in the Kebra Nagast (ch. 11) with the Virgin Mary, the heavenly and spiritual Zion.

morph.gloss: this is he, that is (he who)

glossary.ወዜና: ዜና — and the account, story, report
glossary.ዘ፫፻፲ወ፰: ፫፻፲ወ፰ — 318, spoken ሠለስቱ ምእት ወዐሠርቱ ወሰመንቱ (śälästu məʾət wä-ʿäśärtu wä-sämäntu) 'three hundred and eighteen'; the Orthodox Fathers of Nicaea
glossary.ርቱዓነ: ርቱዕ — orthodox, upright — construct pl. 'orthodox of'
glossary.ወፈድፋደሰ: ፈድፋደ — and especially, and the more so
glossary.ለሊሁ: ለሊሁ — he himself, his very self
glossary.ጽርሐ: ጽርሕ — hall, chamber, inner sanctuary (construct; Budge 'fortress')
glossary.ኵሉ: ኵሉ — all, every, the whole of
glossary.ወበሥምረት: ሥምረት — and with good will, agreement
glossary.ወበዕሪና: ዕሪና — and with concord, being of one accord
glossary.ገብርዋ: ገብረ — they made it/her (root g-b-r, 3pl + obj)
glossary.ወሠምሩ: ሠምረ — and they consented, were pleased (root ś-m-r)
glossary.እለብስ: ለብሰ — I will put on, clothe myself (imperf. 1s, l-b-s)
glossary.አኀድር: ኀደረ — I will dwell, abide (imperf. 1s, ḫ-d-r)
glossary.ወገብሮ: ገብረ — and he made him (g-b-r + 3ms obj)
glossary.በዘዚአሁ: ዚአ — in that which is his own, after his own
glossary.ይንሥቶ: ነሥአ — that he might remove/take him (n-ś-ʾ + obj)
glossary.ወያቅሞ: አቀመ — and that he might establish him (q-w-m caus. + obj)
glossary.ኀራን: ኄር — good, upright, honest; here masc. plural ኄራን 'the good/righteous' (ኀ/ኄ manuscript variant; Dillmann)
glossary.እከውን: ኮነ — I will become, be (imperf. 1s, k-w-n)
glossary.ወኣስተርኢ: አስተርአየ — and I will appear, reveal myself (r-ʾ-y caus.)
glossary.እትገሰስ: ገሰሰ — I will be touched/handled, made palpable (g-s-s)
glossary.ወበደኃሪ: ደኃሪ — and in the latter, last
glossary.ዝውእቱ: ውእቱ — this is he, that is (he who)
